$description: YSS (Yogoda Satsanga Society of India) visual language. SCAFFOLD — awaiting YSS editorial input (PRO-043). Values derived from yssofindia.org analysis (March 2026). YSS is a sister organization to SRF, not a subsidiary. This design language is independent — informed by shared lineage but expressing a distinctly Indian aesthetic tradition. Metaphor: entering an ashram — devotional warmth, Indian earth tones, the red soil of Ranchi.
$deprecated: Draft — awaiting YSS editorial input. Do not treat these values as finalized.

color.$description: YSS color palette. Terracotta and earth tones replace navy and gold. The warmth of Indian earth, not the cool depth of Pacific contemplation. Values extracted from yssofindia.org; require YSS approval before production use.

terracotta.$description: YSS Terracotta. The rust-red earth of Ranchi, the clay of Indian tradition. Primary accent. Equivalent role to SRF Gold but warmer, earthier.

earth-dark.$description: Deep earth. Primary text color. Warm dark brown, not cold black. The ink of handwritten Sanskrit manuscripts.

clay.$description: Warm clay. Primary background. The sun-baked walls of an ashram courtyard.

clay-dark.$description: Secondary clay. Differentiation surface. Deeper warmth.

surface.$description: Elevated surface. Warmer than the SRF equivalent — even 'white' is warm here.

saffron.$description: Saffron accent. PROVISIONAL — may replace or complement terracotta for spiritual emphasis. The color of renunciation in Indian tradition.
saffron.$deprecated: Provisional — requires YSS input on whether saffron is appropriate for their digital presence.

typography.$description: YSS type system. Hindi/Devanagari-primary for Indian audiences, with English as secondary. Raleway for UI (from yssofindia.org). Noto Serif Devanagari for Hindi reading text (ADR-080).

reading-hi.$description: Hindi reading font. Noto Serif Devanagari at 20px (11% larger than Latin 18px). 1.9 line height for shirorekha (headline) clearance. ADR-080.

reading-en.$description: English reading font for YSS context. Same as SRF — Merriweather for consistency across the lineage.

ui.$description: UI font. Raleway from yssofindia.org. Geometric sans-serif — more modern than Open Sans, reflecting YSS's contemporary digital presence.

ui-hi.$description: Hindi UI font. Noto Sans (not Serif) for UI chrome — cleaner at small sizes. Slightly larger than Latin UI to match optical weight.

theme.$description: YSS themes. SCAFFOLD — only earth theme exists as proof-of-concept in the portal. Full YSS theme set needs YSS input.

earth.$description: The primary YSS theme. Warm clay and terracotta. Already implemented as portal's 'earth' theme.

night.$description: SCAFFOLD. YSS dark theme — needs design. Should be warm dark (deep brown/maroon), not cool dark (navy). The darkness of a temple interior at evening aarti.

motifs.$description: YSS visual motifs. SCAFFOLD. YSS uses different ornamental traditions than SRF — Indian geometric patterns, rangoli-inspired dividers, possibly ashram imagery. Requires YSS creative input.
motifs.$deprecated: Scaffold — no YSS motif assets created yet.

languages.$description: YSS serves 9 Indian languages. The design system must support all Indic scripts from yssofindia.org: Hindi, Bengali, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Odia, Assamese, Marathi, Malayalam.
